market.title: India-US BTA: July 24 tariff expiry deadline creates urgency — deal 'very, very close' but unsigned; Section 301 results key
market.detail: USTR Greer and Commerce Minister Goyal concluded talks June 24 without signing. Goyal stated June 26 (London) that India will only sign if it gets competitive tariff advantage over ASEAN rivals — key condition post-US Supreme Court ruling that invalidated reciprocal tariffs. US temporary 10% tariffs expire July 24. Post-expiry, only MFN tariffs apply (higher for Indian exports) unless a deal is in place. US Section 301 investigation results expected next month — outcome could provide a new tariff framework enabling the deal. Marco Rubio (June 27) said India-US deal is in its 'last inches'. Deal signing would be the largest near-term catalyst for FII equity re-entry, especially into IT and pharma.

market.title: Jio Platforms + NSE IPO: India's capital market depth event of the decade — both DRHPs filed, listings targeting Oct-Nov 2026
market.detail: Jio Platforms DRHP filed June 19 (27 crore fresh shares, ~₹37,700 crore / ~$4.5B raise, valuation $131–180B). NSE DRHP filed June 17 (OFS ~6%, valuation >₹5 lakh crore). Jio proceeds earmarked for RJIL debt repayment. NSE listing target Navratri–Diwali 2026. Both IPOs would surpass Hyundai India's ₹27,870 crore (India's current record). Together they create two new mega-cap investable entities: India's dominant telecom/digital platform and its primary capital markets infrastructure. FII demand for these listings will require significant new India equity allocation, supporting the broader market.

market.title: Monsoon 46% deficit — driest June in 146 years — primary macro risk to H2 FY27 growth, food inflation and rural demand
market.detail: India received only 53.1 mm rainfall June 4–22 vs 97.6 mm historical benchmark — a 46% deficit. IMD June 25: conditions improving for monsoon advance into Gujarat, MP, Chhattisgarh, Jharkhand, Bihar, parts of UP/Uttarakhand over next 3–4 days. NE India recording heavy rainfall but West and Central India remain deficient. Cumulative deficit risk to: (1) Kharif crop sowing — key period mid-June to mid-July; (2) Food inflation spike threatening CPI above 4% in Q2 FY27; (3) Rural FMCG/two-wheeler demand in H2; (4) Agri-NBFC credit quality. IMD full-season forecast is 'above normal' but June start is the weakest in 146 years. Critical monitoring period: July 1–15 for kharif sowing progress.

market.title: FII June MTD net selling ₹45,122 crore vs DII buying ₹76,156 crore; June 22 FII bulk buy ₹5,527 crore signals potential reversal
market.detail: FII/FPI net sold ₹45,122 crore in June 2026 through June 25. DII net bought ₹76,156 crore in the same period — DII absorption at 1.69x FII outflows, cushioning markets above 23,800–24,000. The June 22 single-day FII net buy of ₹5,527 crore was the largest since February 3 (₹5,236 crore). India VIX at 13.84 on June 29 (+6%) signals rising near-term hedging activity. Q1 FY27 earnings season (starts July) and July 24 BTA deadline are the next two macro triggers that will determine FII direction. A clean BTA signing + positive Q1 results would likely trigger a 3–4% Nifty upside.
market.source: Moneycontrol FII/DII activity table (NSE cash, month-to-date)

market.title: Market closed June 26 (Muharram); 4-day week ending June 25 at Sensex 77,100 / Nifty 24,056; June 29 post-Muharram profit booking
market.detail: Indian equity market was closed on June 26 for Muharram holiday. The week of June 22-25 (4 trading days) saw Sensex close at 77,100.47 (+109 pts, +0.14%) and Nifty at 24,056 (+34 pts, +0.14%) on June 25 — third consecutive weekly advance underpinned by Brent crude below $80, BTA optimism, and FII June 22 bulk buying. June 29 (Monday post-holiday) saw Nifty fall -110 pts to 23,946 on profit booking. India VIX up 6% on June 29 suggests increased options hedging ahead of Q1 FY27 earnings season. Nifty technical: 23,800 is key support; 24,200 is immediate resistance.

catalysts.title: Accenture revenue forecast cut June 19 triggers IT sector fall; Persistent -10% on Nagarro acquisition; BTA + Q1 FY27 are recovery catalysts
catalysts.detail: Nifty IT fell 3.65% on June 19 after Accenture lowered its annual revenue growth forecast — a global bellwether for Indian IT demand. Nifty IT down another 1% on June 29. Sector still ~27% below 2025 highs. Persistent Systems fell 10.36% on June 29 on Nagarro acquisition concerns (€81/share, 140% premium, $1.4B EV at 9.6x EV/EBITDA). Positive offset: Hexaware +6% as Anthropic-authorised Claude reseller on Amazon Bedrock. Three remaining catalysts for sector re-rating: (1) BTA signing before July 24 (removes Section 301 tariff overhang); (2) Q1 FY27 results in July showing AI deal revenue; (3) FII structural re-entry into large-cap IT. India VIX at 13.84 reflects elevated short-term IT hedging. HCLTech differentiated via Sarvam AI acquisition.

catalysts.title: Kotak CEO succession + HDFC Bank chairman search + RBI August rate cut path — binary outcomes for banking sector re-rating
catalysts.detail: Kotak Mahindra Bank CEO Ashok Vaswani won't seek reappointment after Dec 31, 2026; Board succession process started — creates near-term governance overhang (Jefferies Buy, TP ₹450). HDFC Bank board to finalise new Chairman within 7–10 days; JPMorgan Overweight TP ₹990 citing multi-year low P/BV and RBI FCNR measures for deposit growth (RBI extended Keki Mistry's term 3 months). Positive: August MPC rate cut (Aug 3–5) near-certain given Brent at $72/bbl; FII bond inflows at 16-month highs in June. IIFL Finance approved ₹10,000 crore fundraise. PSU Bank index -1.21% on June 29 on credit growth uncertainty. Rate-cut cycle is the single most powerful banking re-rating catalyst: improves NIM trajectory, bond portfolios (MTM gains), and NBFC cost of funds.

catalysts.title: Pharma outperforms on June 29 (+1.09%); Lupin USFDA tentative approval Enzalutamide; Dr Reddy's Form 483 manageable — sector defensive amid macro uncertainty
catalysts.detail: Nifty Pharma rose 1.09% on June 29 — sole major sectoral gainer alongside Metals and Healthcare — while Nifty fell 0.46%. Lupin received USFDA tentative approval for Enzalutamide (40–160 mg, prostate cancer). Dr Reddy's gained 2.30% (top Nifty gainer); Nomura retained Buy TP ₹1,740 post Form 483 (Hyderabad inspection, 7 observations on new facility — manageable, final approval Q4 FY27). Ajanta Pharma +6.36% (52-week high). Zydus-Sunshine JV for Sri Lanka pharma manufacturing ($20M). BTA pharma tariff clause remains key: US Section 301 investigation on pharma could be resolved in July 2026 alongside broader deal framework.

catalysts.title: Nifty Auto -2% on June 29; M&M -2.83%, IndiGo sharply lower; monsoon deficit threatens rural two-wheeler demand in H2 FY27
catalysts.detail: Nifty Auto fell 2.06% on June 29 — the worst-performing sector. M&M -2.83%, Kotak Bank -2.71%, IndiGo, Eicher Motors, and Bajaj Auto sharply lower. The auto sector faces near-term headwinds from: (1) Monsoon deficit threatening rural two-wheeler demand (Hero MotoCorp, TVS, Bajaj Auto); (2) JLR FCF concerns weighing on Tata Motors; (3) profit booking after sector's strong recovery from March lows. Positive offset: Delhi EV policy (effective July 1) provides a structural catalyst for EV leaders. Tata Motors and Castrol India signed MoU for used oil circularity ecosystem in Karnataka.

catalysts.title: Torrent Power completes Nabha acquisition ₹3,632 cr; Power Grid exclusion order against KEC International revoked — structural power demand upcycle intact
catalysts.detail: Torrent Power completed acquisition of Nabha Power (1,400 MW thermal, Punjab) from L&T Power Development for ₹3,632.35 crore on June 29 — expands Torrent's generation capacity base. Power Grid revoked exclusion order against KEC International (effective June 26), allowing it to bid for Power Grid tenders immediately — positive for KEC order inflows. India's structural electricity demand upcycle from data centres, AI infrastructure, EV charging, and industrial expansion remains intact. Brent crude at $72/bbl reduces thermal power generation costs. August rate cut (if confirmed) lowers cost of capital for regulated infra assets.
catalysts.source: Moneycontrol / Power Grid BSE filing — June 29, 2026

catalysts.title: Nifty Realty -0.93% on June 29 on profit booking; Oberoi launches ultra-luxury Gurugram project — rate-cut thesis intact but near-term consolidation
catalysts.detail: Nifty Realty slipped 0.91% on June 29 after three weeks of sharp outperformance driven by rate-cut expectations. Oberoi Realty launched Three Sixty North at Sector 58, Golf Course Extension Road, Gurugram (14.8 acres); stock rose 1.31% on the day — demonstrates continued premium residential demand. With Brent at $72/bbl and 10Y G-sec at ~6.8%, the August rate cut thesis is intact. However, monsoon deficit raises food inflation risk — a potential CPI overshoot would push back RBI rate cut expectations and reset the sector's valuation premium. DLF, Prestige, Godrej Properties consolidating after the June 13–17 surge.

catalysts.title: HAL FY26 200% dividend; Apollo Micro Systems +6% on BEL QRSAM ₹30,000 crore order expectation; sector consolidates post peace-deal re-rating
catalysts.detail: HAL board approved ₹10/share final dividend for FY26 (200% of face value). Apollo Micro Systems rose ~6% on expectation that BEL is set to receive a ₹30,000 crore QRSAM (Quick Reaction Surface-to-Air Missile) order — largest single defence contract expected in FY27. HAL stock -1.51% and BEL -1.7% on June 29 as the sector consolidates after the war-premium unwind. India's domestic defence capex (₹6.22 lakh crore FY25-30) and AMCA programme remain structural catalysts. AMCA RFP responses due July will be the next major headline.

catalysts.title: Airtel leads subscriber additions in May; Jio DRHP unlocks standalone valuation; Jio FY26 ARPU ₹214, revenues ₹1.47 lakh crore
catalysts.detail: TRAI May 2026 data: Bharti Airtel added 29.26 lakh subscribers in May (vs 31.39 lakh April); Jio added 21.54 lakh (vs 29.42 lakh April); Vodafone Idea +1.21 lakh. Airtel maintained subscriber addition lead — positive for ARPU trajectory. Jio Platforms DRHP (filed June 19): FY26 revenues ₹1.47 lakh crore, EBITDA ₹76,255 crore (margin 51.9%), PAT ~₹30,000 crore, 52.44 crore subscribers, ARPU ₹214. The DRHP filing unlocks Jio's standalone valuation ($131–180B) and creates the basis for RIL's sum-of-parts re-rating. Jio's international technology stack (5G, FWA, AI services) positioned for export partnerships.

stocks.catalyst: Nagarro acquisition at 140% premium ($1.4B EV) + Nagarro growth lagging Persistent organic — deal economics questioned; stock -10.36%
stocks.detail: Persistent Systems signed SPA to acquire 100% of Nagarro SE at €81/share (21% from Lantano Beteiligungen, then full 100%); deal at $1.4B EV / 1.2x EV/sales / 9.6x EV/EBITDA; 140% premium to pre-deal Nagarro price. Nagarro's growth trails Persistent's organic growth rate. CLSA retains 'high conviction outperform' TP ₹6,520 (~6% EPS accretion). Separately secured $650M US tech services deal. Settlement Q4 CY2026–Q1 CY2027. Near-term: deal complexity, integration uncertainty, premium absorption pressure.

stocks.catalyst: CEO Ashok Vaswani won't seek reappointment post Dec 31; succession process started — leadership uncertainty overhang; stock -2.71%
stocks.detail: Ashok Vaswani has informed the Board he does not wish to seek reappointment after his current term ends Dec 31, 2026. Board NRC has begun CEO succession process — candidate will need RBI approval (typically 3-6 months). Jefferies retained Buy TP ₹450 noting senior management has changed significantly since founder Uday Kotak's exit; stock +11% over 3 years. Near-term: leadership uncertainty + CEO transition typically creates 3-6 months of institutional underweight positioning until successor clarity.

stocks.catalyst: S&P credit upgrade to BBB (from BBB-) with stable outlook — lowers cost of international debt; governance premium re-rated
stocks.detail: S&P Global Ratings upgraded APSEZ long-term issuer credit rating and senior unsecured notes to BBB from BBB- with stable outlook on June 29. Upgrade reflects improved financial profile, reduced group-level governance concerns, and APSEZ's execution on deleveraging. BBB rating opens access to wider global institutional bond demand (many mandates require investment-grade BBB minimum). Adani Mundra Airport commissioned as multi-modal logistics hub (June 23). APSEZ stock at ₹1,783 on June 29.

stocks.catalyst: Named Anthropic-authorised reseller for Claude on Amazon Bedrock — differentiates in enterprise AI; stock +6.16% to ₹525
stocks.detail: Hexaware Technologies named an Anthropic-authorised reseller for Claude AI models on Amazon Bedrock — joins a select group globally authorised to sell, integrate, and support Claude directly for enterprise clients worldwide. This differentiates Hexaware from generic IT services players and enables higher-margin AI deployment contracts. Stock rose 6.16% — top BSE midcap gainer on June 29 in a broadly negative market. Contrast to Nifty IT -1.10% on the same day highlights the AI-capable vs AI-resistant IT services divergence.

stocks.catalyst: Board to finalise new Chairman within 7-10 days; JPMorgan Overweight TP ₹990 at multi-year low P/BV; RBI FCNR measures support deposit growth
stocks.detail: HDFC Bank board expected to finalise new full-time Chairman within 7–10 days (NRC submitting 3-candidate panel; Chairman then recommends CEO to RBI). RBI extended interim Chairman Keki Mistry's tenure 3 months in June. JPMorgan retains Overweight TP ₹990 citing: (1) legal review should reduce governance risk premium; (2) stock de-rated 8% on P/BV since former chairman's resignation; (3) RBI's FCNR measures to boost deposits and loan growth; (4) multi-year low valuation supports upside. Resolution of chairman overhang is the near-term re-rating catalyst. Stock at ₹797 on June 29.

stocks.catalyst: Jio DRHP filed June 19 with SEBI — $131-180B standalone valuation unlocked; RIL sum-of-parts discount to narrow materially
stocks.detail: Jio Platforms DRHP filed June 19 — fresh issue 27 crore shares (~2.9% float), target raise ₹37,700 crore (~$4.5B), proceeds earmarked for RJIL debt repayment. Valuation range $131B (JPMorgan) to $180B (Jefferies). Jio FY26: revenues ₹1.47 lakh crore, EBITDA margin 51.9%, ARPU ₹214, 52.44 crore subscribers. RIL holds 66.43%; Meta 9.98%; Google 7.73% (none selling in IPO). Listing would narrow the chronic RIL sum-of-parts discount. O2C segment benefits from Brent at $72/bbl (lower feedstock cost). Akash, Isha, Anant Ambani leading the Jio IPO process.

stocks.catalyst: USFDA tentative approval for Enzalutamide (40–160 mg) prostate cancer drug; pharma sector outperformance on June 29 (+1.09%)
stocks.detail: Lupin received USFDA tentative approval for Enzalutamide Tablets in 40 mg, 80 mg, 120 mg, and 160 mg strengths. Drug used to treat multiple types of prostate cancer (a large US market segment). Final approval and launch pending patent/exclusivity resolution. Stock rose 1.97% to ₹2,388 on June 29. Pharma sector outperformed on June 29 (+1.09% Nifty Pharma) as defensive accumulation offset broader market weakness. BTA pharma provisions (Section 301 resolution) remain a key catalyst for the sector.

stocks.catalyst: Top Nifty gainer +2.30% on June 29; Nomura Buy TP ₹1,740 maintained post USFDA Form 483 — manageable observations
stocks.detail: Dr Reddy's Laboratories was the top Nifty 50 gainer on June 29 (+2.30% to ~₹1,330+). USFDA issued 7 Form 483 observations post-Hyderabad inspection — mostly related to new facility; company expects final approval and launch in Q4 FY27. Nomura retained Buy TP ₹1,740 — 7 observations on a new facility is considered procedural, not a systemic Warning Letter-level issue. DRL is a key defensive pharma holding with strong US generics and branded India business. Pharma sector remains the key defensive sector in the current market.

stocks.catalyst: US CBP ruling confirms no adverse inference on Chinese-origin solar cell allegation — US operations and manufacturing unaffected
stocks.detail: Waaree Energies received a clean US Customs and Border Protection (CBP) determination: CBP confirmed that Waaree did NOT export solar modules manufactured using Chinese-origin solar cells to the United States; CBP drew no adverse inference; ruling is limited to a narrow subset of certain historical import entries. US manufacturing (4.2 GW domestic US capacity) and commercial operations continue normally. Removes a key regulatory overhang for Waaree's US business expansion. Critical positive given the US solar tariff environment.

stocks.catalyst: Delhi EV policy effective July 1 — electric auto-rickshaws only from Jan 2027; EV two-wheelers mandatory from April 2028; stock +7.99% on June 29
stocks.detail: Ather Energy rose 7.99% to ₹1,078.60 on June 29 — top Nifty Smallcap 100 gainer — following Delhi Cabinet's new EV policy approval. Policy: electric auto-rickshaws only eligible for new registrations from January 1, 2027; EV two-wheeler registrations mandatory from April 1, 2028; scrapping incentive ₹1 lakh for BS-IV four-wheelers. Delhi is India's largest two-wheeler and auto-rickshaw market. Ather Energy is the premium electric two-wheeler brand (Ather 450, Rizta series) with strong Delhi market presence. Policy provides a hard demand deadline catalyst.

stocks.catalyst: JLR 'close to zero' FCF pressure continues; Tata Motors-Castrol MoU for used oil circularity; domestic EV tailwind from Delhi policy
stocks.detail: Tata Motors stock declined 1.69% on June 29. JLR 'close to zero' FY26 FCF guidance (reiterated June 16-17) continues to weigh on the investment thesis — JLR contributes ~70% of revenues. India domestic PV business remains strong; Tata Motors-Castrol signed MoU June 29 for used oil circularity pilot in Karnataka (authorised service network). Delhi EV policy (mandatory EV two-wheelers from April 2028) is a positive for Tata's Nexon EV and Tiago EV. Net: JLR FCF recovery timeline in FY27 is the key re-rating event. Watch FY27 Q1 JLR FCF guidance in July.
